channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The Latest: Harris continues media blitz with 3 more national interviews
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is continuing her media tour Tuesday with three more interviews for national audiences, with Election Day now just four weeks away.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As FEMA prepares for Hurricane Milton, it battles rumors surrounding Helene recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rumors surrounding Hurricane Helene are many. There are false claims that people taking federal relief money could see their land seized. Or that $750 is the most they will ever get to rebuild.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military board substantiates misconduct but declines to fire Marine who adopted Afghan orphan
item.description: CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C. (AP) — A U.S. Marine whose adoption of an Afghan war orphan has spurred a yearslong legal battle and raised alarms at the highest levels of government will remain on active duty. A three-member panel of Marines found Tuesday that while Maj.
item.dc:creator: JULIET LINDERMAN, CLAIRE GALOFARO and MARTHA MENDOZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump calls Maine Gov. Janet Mills a man in a mistake-riddled call to supporters, newspaper reports
item.description: Republican Donald Trump mixed up Democratic Gov. Janet Mills’ gender on a phone call with supporters in Maine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fox News Politics: Harris on '60 minutes': Joe 2.0?
item.description: The latest updates from the 2024 campaign trail, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court signals support for Biden administration regulation of 'ghost gun' kits
item.description: Supreme Court signals support for Biden administration regulation of "ghost gun" ready-to-assemble kits in Garland v. VanDerStok (23-852). A ruling is expected by summer 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lombardo among GOP governors criticizing Biden-Harris immigration asylum actions
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo was among 25 Republican governors who signed a letter criticizing the Biden-Harris administration over asylum policies that have brought more than a half-million immigrants into the United States.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate live: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: With no government shutdown, it feels like Christmas in September
item.description: This spending bill funds the government through October, November and just before Christmas, setting up another showdown just before Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Walz appeared on ABC's 'The View' and 'Jimmy Kimmel Live'
item.description: The Democratic presidential ticket is on a media blitz in New York. Vice President Kamala Harris appeared Tuesday on ABC's “The View," and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz appeared on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Overseas meth sent to US election building shuts down office for hours
item.description: The Dutchess County Board of Elections office shut down for hours Monday over a suspicious package from Europe containing crystal meth, according to a press release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New poll shows Harris taking a slim lead over Trump thanks to support from a surprising group
item.description: Poll indicates increased support from Republicans is helping fuel Vice President Kamala Harris with 4 weeks to go until Election Day in her White House showdown with former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moreno looks to close the gap on Sherrod Brown in Ohio
item.description: Bernie Moreno and Republicans are attempting to narrow the gap against Sen. Sherrod Brown (D) in Ohio as they try to nab one of the most contested races in the country in a battle for the Senate majority.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Harris and Trump mark somber anniversary of Hamas’ Oct. 7 attack on Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Donald Trump and Vice President Kamala Harris marked the anniversary of the deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust as the presidential candidates approach the final weeks of the campaign during a widening conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, COLLEEN LONG and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rafael Arroyo discusses wasteful spending, studio tax credits, and California's policies in Nevada
item.description: Rafael Arroyo, the Republican candidate running for Assembly District 41, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide, outlining his vision and priorities as the election approaches.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump: Israel has to 'get smart' about supporting him
item.description: Former President Trump said Monday Israel has to “get smart” about supporting him and suggested Jewish people have not adequately backed him after he took several pro-Israel actions while in office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris' and Donald Trump's economic plans.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Deputy Mayor for Public Safety Philip Banks resigns
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Deputy Mayor for Public Safety Philip Banks III has resigned from his position, his spokespeople said on Monday. The resignation comes after sources said Friday that First Deputy Mayor Sheena Wright was also expected to resign.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Can you stop calls and texts from political campaigns?
item.description: If you've received a political text or call recently, you may be wondering how to make it stop, or what you can do to prevent them in the future.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump urges his supporters to deliver victory in his return to scene of first assassination attempt
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump returned on Saturday to the Pennsylvania fairgrounds where he was nearly assassinated in July, urging a large crowd to deliver an Election Day victory that he tied to his survival of the shooting.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, JILL COLVIN and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Harris pledges ongoing federal support as she visits North Carolina to survey Helene's aftermath
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris pledged ongoing federal support and praised the “heroes among us” as she visited North Carolina on Saturday in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, her second trip in four days to the disaster zone.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How the Butler shooting changed Donald Trump's campaign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he had spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: NYPD Chief John Chell discusses security plans for Oct. 7
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Monday marks one year since Hamas’ deadly attack on Israel. On Oct. 7, 2023, Hamas-led militants killed some 1,200 people, mostly civilians, in an attack in southern Israel and took around 250 hostage. Some 100 are still held, around 65 of whom are believed to be alive.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Politics over Humanity? How Hurricane Helene impacts the political field
item.description: Politics over Humanity? How Hurricane Helene impacts the Political field as the race to the White House is weeks away.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: In excerpt from new memoir, Melania Trump says women have the ‘right to choose’ abortion
item.description: The former first lady says she is a longtime supporter of abortion rights. Her memoir is coming out a year after former President Donald Trump said he was "able to kill Roe v. Wade.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are some important election deadlines for the Las Vegas valley
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Nevada voters have until Oct. 22 to register to vote should they want a mail-in ballot, and remember, in-person registration is available on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC
